title: Middleware Auth
description: Auth policy must apply uniformly before any `/v1` handler runs. Middleware holds the verifier resolved at startup so v1 local mode is pass-through while future types can reject unauthenticated calls with RFC 7807 problems.
context: Auth policy must apply uniformly before any `/v1` handler runs. Middleware holds the verifier resolved at startup so v1 local mode is pass-through while future types can reject unauthenticated calls with RFC 7807 problems.

rules: The middleware MUST use the `ApiTokenVerifier` instance produced by `auth-adapter-registry.resolve` at server startup and MUST NOT construct verifier implementations inline.

rules.requirement: disabled auth never returns 401 for missing credentials
rules: When effective `api.auth.type` is `disabled`, missing or empty `Authorization` MUST NOT produce HTTP 401.

rules.requirement: middleware attaches identity to request context
rules: After verification (or pass-through), middleware MUST attach the resolved identity to the object returned by `createApiContext` so mutating handlers pass the correct actor into kernel use cases.

rules.requirement: enforcing auth types return 401 problem+json on failure
rules: When a future registered auth type requires credentials and `verify` fails, middleware MUST respond with HTTP 401 and `application/problem+json` without invoking route handlers.

constraints: HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.
